title: Config sync in 4 steps
subtitle: Annotate a ConfigMap or Secret and let Config Syncer keep every copy in sync — across namespaces or entire clusters.

steps.title: Install the Operator
steps.description: Deploy Config Syncer via Helm into any Kubernetes cluster — EKS, GKE, AKS, or on-prem. One command, a few minutes.

steps.title: Annotate Your Config
steps.description: Add the kubed.appscode.com/sync annotation to any ConfigMap or Secret to opt it into syncing — to all namespaces, or a label-selected subset.

steps.title: Sync Across Clusters
steps.description: Point at remote cluster contexts in a kubeconfig with the sync-contexts annotation to replicate the same ConfigMap or Secret into other clusters.

steps.title: Stay in Sync Automatically
steps.description: Updates to the source propagate to every copy instantly. Remove the annotation or delete the source, and the copies clean themselves up.
